title: oberonia bertoldi
intro: Oberonia bertoldi, also known as the Indian orchid or wild orchid, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant native to the Western Ghats in India. It is a type of orchid that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Oberonia bertoldi include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, as well as its use in treating wounds and skin conditions. Oberonia bertoldi is typically prepared in herbal form as a decoction or infusion, and it is often used in Ayurvedic medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
